title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Injection, Response Validation, and Least Authority · Automation and Data Client Systems · Contract Workshop
summary: A civic-data automation team integrating a staging catalog must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for ssrf, dns rebinding, injection, response validation, and least authority, producing defense-in-depth outbound request policy. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers defense-in-depth outbound request policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction…
objectives: Allow-list required schemes, ports, hosts, and destinations and enforce network egress limits for server-side fetching. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Address DNS rebinding and validation-to-connect races by binding policy to resolved and connected destinations. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Reject control characters and unsafe dynamic structure in targets and fields and rely on maintained encoders. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ssrf Destination Policy
steps.instruction:
workshop case — civic-data staging importer receives https://127.0.0.1:8006/meta, a decimal IP alias, a hostname that rebinds, and a redirect to file://. Enforce scheme, resolution, connected-address, redirect, response, egress, and privacy defenses.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ssrf Destination Policy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for allow-list required schemes, ports, hosts, and destinations and enforce network egress limits for server-side fetching. demonstrate the result through python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports. For Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ssrf Destination Policy, keep Ssrf Destination Policy connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
